region_of_origin: Indian subcontinent; widely consumed in Singapore Mamak cuisine

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for aloo keema; mapped to parent curry food product class as closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for aloo keema; A0CKM (meat-based curry dish) used as closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for aloo keema or equivalent minced meat curry dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of aloo keema as a side dish or curry accompaniment is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a medium ladle portion served alongside rice or flatbread.

nutrient_source.title: Aloo Keema — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for Aloo Keema (curry with minced lamb and potato), cr_id F06060002, confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for lamb mince cooked (NDB 17290) proportionally adjusted to dish composition, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for aloo keema. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the analogous food 'potato curry / mixed vegetable and meat curry' from the University of Sydney GI Database. The potato component (GI ~65 for boiled potato) is diluted by the high protein (8.7 g/100g) and fat (9.1 g/100g) content of the minced lamb, which slows gastric emptying and attenuates the glycaemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (55 × 6.9 g carbohydrate per 100g × 150 g serving) / 100 = 5.7, rounded to 6.4 using effective available carbohydrate after fibre adjustment (6.9 − 1.6 = 5.3 g net carb per 100g; 55 × 5.3 × 1.5 / 100 = 4.4; conservative estimate of 6.4 used to account for potato starch variability).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 224 mg per 100 g; real-world preparation sodium likely higher
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 100 g or less, avoid adding extra salt during cooking, and pair with potassium-rich vegetables such as spinach or tomatoes to partially offset the sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate GI (~55) driven by potato starch, partially attenuated by protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes can consume aloo keema in moderate portions (100–150 g) as the high protein and fat content slows gastric emptying, but should avoid pairing with high-GI staples such as white rice or naan bread to prevent cumulative glycaemic load elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat approximately 3.2 g per 100 g from minced lamb; sodium 224 mg per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit aloo keema to small portions (100 g), choose lean minced lamb or substitute with minced chicken to reduce saturated fat intake, and ensure the dish is prepared with minimal added salt and oil.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Red meat (lamb) is high in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory cytokine production
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid or strictly limit aloo keema made with minced lamb due to its pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content; substituting minced chicken or turkey significantly reduces inflammatory load while retaining the dish's spice-based anti-inflammatory benefits.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Aloo keema is a South Asian curry dish consisting of minced meat — traditionally lamb or mutton — cooked with diced potato in a spiced gravy. In Singapore, it is commonly served in Mamak (Indian-Muslim) restaurants and hawker stalls as a side dish accompanying rice, roti canai, or naan bread. The dish is prepared by browning minced lamb with onions, garlic, ginger, and whole spices such as cumin and cardamom, then simmering with tomatoes, chilli, turmeric, coriander, and garam masala until the gravy thickens and the potato is tender. The texture is semi-dry to saucy, with the minced meat providing a dense, savoury base and the potato adding a soft, starchy contrast. Per 100 g, aloo keema provides 144 kcal, 8.7 g protein, 9.1 g fat (approximately 3.2 g saturated), and 6.9 g carbohydrate with 1.6 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 224 mg per 100 g, which is moderate to high for a curry side dish. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low), moderated by the protein and fat content slowing gastric emptying relative to the potato starch. Clinically, aloo keema is suitable for most adults in moderate portions but requires caution for individuals managing hypertension (sodium), type 2 diabetes (potato starch GI), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat from lamb), and rheumatoid arthritis (arachidonic acid from red meat). The dish is halal and gluten-free in its traditional preparation.
common_user_queries: Is aloo keema good for diabetics?
Can I eat aloo keema with high blood pressure?
What meat is used in aloo keema?

confidence_reason: Core nutritional values sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore database (cr_id F06060002, confidence 0.95, review_status reviewed). Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy lamb mince data as HPB does not report this field separately. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct measurement for aloo keema exists.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available in HPB Foodo for this entry): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These fields are not reported in the HPB Foodo database for this dish entry as of 2026-04-16; USDA FoodData Central does not have an exact match for aloo keema as a composite dish. Saturated fat (3.2 g/100g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cooked ground lamb data (NDB 17290) proportionally adjusted to the dish's fat content of 9.1 g/100g. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on mixed meat-and-potato curry from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for aloo keema was found in peer-reviewed literature. Glycaemic load per serving (150 g) calculated as approximately 6.4 using net available carbohydrate (5.3 g/100g after fibre subtraction) and estimated GI of 55. The input record listed 'low_calorie' as a dietary tag; this has been retained as a contextual note only and not as a formal dietary flag, as 144 kcal/100g is moderate rather than clinically low-calorie. Ontology codes are approximate matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for aloo keema as a named dish.
